seo.title: Louisiana CNA Certification Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your Certification (2026)
seo.description: Already certified as a CNA? Here's whether your certification transfers to Louisiana - endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: CNA license reciprocity louisiana, how to transfer CNA license to louisiana, louisiana CNA endorsement application

board.name: Louisiana Dept of Health
board.mailing_address: Louisiana Department of Health, Health Standards Section, P. O. Box 3767, Baton Rouge, LA 70821

quick_facts.processing_time: 1 to 2 Weeks after passing the competency exam

license_types.title: Certified Nursing Assistant (CNA)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a nursing assistant in Louisiana.

steps.title: Complete an Approved Training Program
steps.description: Enroll in and successfully complete a Nurse Aide Training and Competency Evaluation Program (NATCEP) approved by the Louisiana Department of Health. This program includes classroom instruction and hands-on clinical experience.

steps.title: Register for the Competency Exam with Pearson VUE
steps.description: Visit the Pearson VUE - Louisiana Nurse Aide Testing website to create an account and register for the Nurse Aide Competency Exam (NACE). You will need to select both the written/oral and skills components of the exam.

steps.title: Schedule and Pass the Competency Exam
steps.description: Schedule your NACE written/oral and skills exams at an approved testing center through your Pearson VUE account. You must pass both parts of the exam to be eligible for certification.

steps.title: Automatic Placement on the Nurse Aide Registry
steps.description: Upon successful completion of both components of the NACE exam, your results will be automatically submitted to the Louisiana Department of Health, Health Standards Section. You will then be placed on the Louisiana Nurse Aide Registry.

steps.title: Receive Confirmation of Certification
steps.description: After placement on the registry, you may receive confirmation of your certification. You can also verify your status anytime using the Louisiana Nurse Aide Registry search tool.

documents.detail: Certificate of completion from your approved CNA training program.
Official verification of passing your state's nurse aide competency evaluation.

provisional_text: Some states allow CNAs to work under supervision for a limited period while their endorsement application is being processed.
reciprocity_text: CNAs certified in another state can often apply via endorsement (reciprocity) by providing registry verification from their home state.

faqs.question: How much does Louisiana CNA endorsement cost?
faqs.answer: The endorsement application fee is $35. Additional costs may include background check fees.

faqs.question: Is a background check required?
faqs.answer: Yes, a fingerprint-based background check is required in Louisiana.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my CNA certification to Louisiana?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 2 to 4 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Louisiana while waiting?
faqs.answer: Check with the state board about temporary practice permits while your endorsement is being processed.

reciprocity.processing_time: 1 to 2 Weeks after passing the competency exam
reciprocity.notes: CNAs certified in another state can often apply via endorsement (reciprocity) by providing registry verification from their home state.

variants.label: New Applicant

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: Transfer an active out-of-state CNA to Louisiana.

hero.title: Become a CNA in Louisiana
hero.dek: To become a Certified Nursing Assistant in Louisiana, you must complete an approved training program and then register for and pass the competency exam administered by Pearson VUE.
